summary: Obfuscated postinstall fetches and executes remote code from onch.cc
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs dist/index.js, a file obfuscated with javascript-obfuscator (hex-named string array, rotation, self-decoding IIFE). When executed with the environment variable P=1, the script issues an HTTPS GET to https://onch.cc/test1.txt (and test2.txt), base64-decodes the response body, and executes it via `new Function('require', decoded)(require)` — arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with the `require` builtin injected, giving whoever controls onch.cc full code execution on the installer's machine. There is no hash, signature, or version pinning on the fetched content; the URL is mutable and can serve different payloads over time. The package's own build script (`javascript-obfuscator./dist/index.js --output./dist/index.js`) confirms the obfuscation is applied deliberately to the install-time payload. The combination of a lifecycle-hook remote fetch from a non-publisher domain, `new Function` execution of the decoded body, and deliberate obfuscation of that lifecycle script is a canonical install-time RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
